title: hemerocallis dumortieri subsp. middendorffii
intro: Hemerocallis dumortieri subsp. middendorffii, also known as the Siberian daylily, is a subspecies of the flowering plant Hemerocallis dumortieri, native to East Asia and parts of Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving rheumatism, and supporting the immune system. It is often used in herbal preparations, including infusions, tinctures, and ointments, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
